title: Unable to Permanently vm.max_map_count = 262144 to Run ElasticSearch on Mac OS
body:
### Bug description

I am unable to permanently set the vm.max_map_count = 262144 value on my Mac while using podman desktop.

While I can run the following commands, the vm.max_map_count value is only set until the next reboot of my mac:
`$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 38.20230527.2.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Wed Jun  7 11:55:40 2023 from 192.168.127.1

$ sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144
vm.max_map_count = 262144

$ exit`

While the above process works.  It doesn't seem to survive restarts of my mac.

Any friendly advice or direction on how to permanently set this value on macs while using podman desktop would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your time.

### Operating system

macOS Monterey 12.6.2 (21G320), Intel

### Version

1.0.x

### Steps to reproduce

`podman run --name es01 --net elastic -p 9200:9200 -it docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.8.0`

### Relevant log output

```shell
{"@timestamp":"2023-06-07T22:11:59.634Z", "log.level":"ERROR", "message":"node validation exception\n[1] bootstrap checks failed. You must address the points described in the following [1] lines before starting Elasticsearch.\nbootstrap check failure [1] of [1]: max virtual memory areas vm.max_map_count [65530] is too low, increase to at least [262144]", "ecs.version": "1.2.0","service.name":"ES_ECS","event.dataset":"elasticsearch.server","process.thread.name":"main","log.logger":"org.elasticsearch.bootstrap.Elasticsearch","elasticsearch.node.name":"0c8cdb21c895","elasticsearch.cluster.name":"docker-cluster"}
```


### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: sysctl's should must set via config file so they will be set on boot, see [`sysctl.conf(5)`](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/sysctl.conf.5.html)

transfering the issue to podman tracker as podman machine is managed there.

about your specific question:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71546876/increase-vm-max-map-count-permanently-in-podman-machine-vm

Inc ase anyone from the future needs the same information and didn't find it clear that podman runs a VM on mac os, it is the podman vm that has to be edited (not your host mac os) to get ElasticSearch running.

Steps...
Login into the Podman VM
From a terminal on your mac:

`$ podman machine ssh`

Edit Default /etc/sysctl.conf File
Using sudo edit the /etc/sysctl.conf and add the vm.max_map_count = 262144 property.

`$ sudo echo "vm.max_map_count = 262144" | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.conf`

After exiting the podman virtual machine you should be able to run the ElasticSearch container:

`$ podman run --name es01 --net elastic -p 9200:9200 -it docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.8.0`

I would then recommend stopping Podman Desktop and rebooting your mac. Then restarting Podman Desktop and trying to run the ElasticSearch container again. It should work.

https://stackoverflow.com/a/76443391/42962
